questions.question: Why does IPFIX / NetFlow v9 use UDP (default port 4739 / 9995) instead of TCP for export?
options: A. UDP is faster than TCP at any speed
B. UDP is stateless; TCP retransmits would double-count in billing, and head-of-line blocking would amplify congestion during incidents
C. UDP is encrypted by default while TCP is plaintext
D. UDP packets are smaller than TCP packets
questions.explanation: Flow export tolerates loss better than reorder or retransmit-duplication. Retransmitting a flow record via TCP would bill the same conversation twice; head-of-line blocking stalls the export pipeline under load. UDP/4739 is deliberate (RFC 7011 §10).

questions.question: What is the role of a NetFlow v9 / IPFIX Template record?
options: A. It contains a single flow's octet and packet counts
B. It declares the field layout (sequence of IANA Information Element IDs and lengths) referenced by subsequent Data Records
C. It negotiates the UDP port for the next flow export
D. It encrypts the exported flow data
questions.explanation: Templates decouple the wire format from the schema. The collector caches (template_id, source_id) -> field layout, then walks Data Records by length, decoding each IE (srcIPv4Address, protocolIdentifier, etc.) from the IANA registry (RFC 7012).

questions.question: At 1-in-1000 random packet sampling, what is the standard-error envelope for an observed octet count?
options: A. Exactly the true value (sampling does not introduce error)
B. sqrt(N) where N is the sample size; the variance scales with the inverse sample rate
C. Proportional to the link MTU
D. Always +50% over the true value
questions.explanation: Random sampling has standard error sqrt(N) for sample size N, so 1-in-1000 over 1,000,000 packets gives N=1000 samples with SE ~31.6 -- roughly a 1-sigma envelope of ~3.16% around the true value. RFC 5475 formalizes this for IPFIX/PSAMP.
